title: Windows Clippy native live tile
summary: Dedicated native WPF widget for the agentcard icon pipeline. The tile reads the adaptive payload, watches for JSON edits, and keeps the icon-generation review surface pinned on the Windows desktop.

generation.promptSummary: Render a clipped capability card mark that stays legible at 32x32 and feels native to Windows Clippy.
generation.negativeConstraints: No text inside the mark, no pre-rounded border, no screenshots, and no low-contrast micro detail.

capabilities: Standalone native WPF window with an always-on-top desktop footprint.
Adaptive-card-backed payload that can be reviewed and edited without reopening the host.
Automatic JSON file watching so the live tile refreshes when icon metadata changes.
Dedicated launcher command that builds the host on demand and opens the tile.

tools.name: clippy-live-tile
tools.purpose: Builds and launches the dedicated native live tile host from the repo root.

tools.name: LiveTileHost
tools.purpose: Standalone WPF renderer for the adaptive live-tile payload and icon assets.

tools.name: dayour-icon
tools.purpose: Turns icon design specs into reviewable prompts, packaged assets, and widget-ready metadata.

tools.name: image_flux_2_pro
tools.purpose: Generates the master square icon candidate through the local llm-lib bridge.

review.summary: The dedicated live tile is wired to the current agentcard icon payload and ready for desktop review.
notes: The host reads the adaptive-card data file directly and refreshes when the JSON changes.
The same icon asset bundle used by the widget schema is surfaced natively in WPF.
The launcher builds LiveTileHost on demand so the repo does not need a separate packaging step.
